21: package org.logstash.common;
22: 
23: import org.jruby.Ruby;
24: import org.jruby.RubyArray;

31: import org.jruby.runtime.builtin.IRubyObject;

33: 
34: @JRubyClass(name = "BufferedTokenizer")
35: public class BufferedTokenizerExt extends RubyObject {
36: 
37:     private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;
38: 
39:     private static final RubyString NEW_LINE = (RubyString) RubyUtil.RUBY.newString("\n").
40:                                                                 freeze(RubyUtil.RUBY.getCurrentContext());
41: 
42:     private @SuppressWarnings("rawtypes") RubyArray input = RubyUtil.RUBY.newArray();
43:     private RubyString delimiter = NEW_LINE;
44:     private int sizeLimit;
45:     private boolean hasSizeLimit;
46:     private int inputSize;
47: 
48:     public BufferedTokenizerExt(final Ruby runtime, final RubyClass metaClass) {
49:         super(runtime, metaClass);
50:     }
51: 
52:     @JRubyMethod(name = "initialize", optional = 2)
53:     public IRubyObject init(final ThreadContext context, IRubyObject[] args) {
54:         if (args.length >= 1) {
55:             this.delimiter = args[0].convertToString();
56:         }
57:         if (args.length == 2) {
58:             final int sizeLimit = args[1].convertToInteger().getIntValue();
59:             if (sizeLimit <= 0) {
60:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException("Size limit must be positive");
61:             }
62:             this.sizeLimit = sizeLimit;
63:             this.hasSizeLimit = true;
64:         }
65:         this.inputSize = 0;
66:         return this;
67:     }
68:

<source_slice path="logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/common/BufferedTokenizerExt.java" lines="100-123">
100:     }
101: 
102:     /**
103:      * Flush the contents of the input buffer, i.e. return the input buffer even though
104:      * a token has not yet been encountered
105:      *
106:      * @param context ThreadContext
107:      * @return Buffer contents
108:      */
109:     @JRubyMethod
110:     public IRubyObject flush(final ThreadContext context) {
111:         final IRubyObject buffer = input.join(context);
112:         input.clear();
113:         inputSize = 0;
114:         return buffer;
115:     }
116: 
117:     @JRubyMethod(name = "empty?")
118:     public IRubyObject isEmpty(final ThreadContext context) {
119:         return RubyUtil.RUBY.newBoolean(input.isEmpty() && (inputSize == 0));
120:     }
121: 
122: }

20: describe  FileWatch::BufferedTokenizer  do
21:   subject { FileWatch::BufferedTokenizer.new }
22:

24:     expect(subject.extract("foo\n")).to eq(["foo"])
25:   end
26: 
27:   it "should merge multiple token" do
28:     expect(subject.extract("foo")).to eq([])
29:     expect(subject.extract("bar\n")).to eq(["foobar"])
30:   end
31: 
32:   it "should tokenize multiple token" do
33:     expect(subject.extract("foo\nbar\n")).to eq(["foo", "bar"])
34:   end
35:

37:     expect(subject.extract("")).to eq([])
38:     expect(subject.extract("foo\nbar")).to eq(["foo"])
39:   end
40:

45:

58:       expect(subject).to be_empty
59:     end

51: 
52:     it "emits the contents of the buffer" do
53:       expect(subject.flush).to eq(data)
54:     end
55: 
56:     it "resets the state of the buffer" do
57:       subject.flush
58:       expect(subject).to be_empty
59:     end
60: 
61:     context 'with decode_size_limit_bytes' do
62:       subject { FileWatch::BufferedTokenizer.new("\n", 100) }
63: 
64:       it "validates size limit" do
65:         expect { FileWatch::BufferedTokenizer.new("\n", -101) }.to raise_error(java.lang.IllegalArgumentException, "Size limit must be positive")
66:         expect { FileWatch::BufferedTokenizer.new("\n", 0) }.to raise_error(java.lang.IllegalArgumentException, "Size limit must be positive")
</source_slice>

<source_slice path="logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/common/BufferedTokenizerExt.java" lines="81-123">
81:     @SuppressWarnings("rawtypes")
82:     public RubyArray extract(final ThreadContext context, IRubyObject data) {
83:         final RubyArray entities = data.convertToString().split(delimiter, -1);
84:         if (hasSizeLimit) {
85:             final int entitiesSize = ((RubyString) entities.first()).size();
86:             if (inputSize + entitiesSize > sizeLimit) {
87:                 throw new IllegalStateException("input buffer full");
88:             }
89:             this.inputSize = inputSize + entitiesSize;
90:         }
91:         input.append(entities.shift(context));
92:         if (entities.isEmpty()) {
93:             return RubyUtil.RUBY.newArray();
94:         }
95:         entities.unshift(input.join(context));
96:         input.clear();
97:         input.append(entities.pop(context));
98:         inputSize = ((RubyString) input.first()).size();
99:         return entities;
100:     }
101: 
102:     /**
103:      * Flush the contents of the input buffer, i.e. return the input buffer even though
104:      * a token has not yet been encountered
105:      *
106:      * @param context ThreadContext
107:      * @return Buffer contents
108:      */
109:     @JRubyMethod
110:     public IRubyObject flush(final ThreadContext context) {
111:         final IRubyObject buffer = input.join(context);
112:         input.clear();
113:         inputSize = 0;
114:         return buffer;
115:     }
116: 
117:     @JRubyMethod(name = "empty?")
118:     public IRubyObject isEmpty(final ThreadContext context) {
119:         return RubyUtil.RUBY.newBoolean(input.isEmpty() && (inputSize == 0));
120:     }
121: 
122: }
123:

81:     @SuppressWarnings("rawtypes")
82:     public RubyArray extract(final ThreadContext context, IRubyObject data) {
83:         final RubyArray entities = data.convertToString().split(delimiter, -1);
84:         if (entities.isEmpty()) {
85:             return RubyUtil.RUBY.newArray();
86:         }
87:         if (hasSizeLimit) {
88:             final int entitiesSize = ((RubyString) entities.first()).size();
89:             if (inputSize + entitiesSize > sizeLimit) {
90:                 throw new IllegalStateException("input buffer full");
91:             }
92:             this.inputSize = inputSize + entitiesSize;
93:         }
94:         input.append(entities.shift(context));
95:         if (entities.isEmpty()) {
96:             return RubyUtil.RUBY.newArray();
97:         }
98:         entities.unshift(input);
99:         input = (RubyString) entities.pop(context);
100:         inputSize = input.size();
101:         return entities;
102:     }
103: 
104:     /**
105:      * Flush the contents of the input buffer, i.e. return the input buffer even though
106:      * a token has not yet been encountered
107:      *
108:      * @param context ThreadContext
109:      * @return Buffer contents
110:      */
111:     @JRubyMethod
112:     public IRubyObject flush(final ThreadContext context) {
113:         final IRubyObject buffer = input;
114:         input = RubyUtil.RUBY.newString();
115:         inputSize = 0;
116:         return buffer;
117:     }
118: 
119:     @JRubyMethod(name = "empty?")
120:     public IRubyObject isEmpty(final ThreadContext context) {
121:         return RubyUtil.RUBY.newBoolean(input.isEmpty() && (inputSize == 0));
122:     }
